extracted_information: The hemp industry in 2024 is undergoing significant state and federal regulatory changes, primarily aimed at restricting intoxicating hemp-derived cannabinoids. The 2018 Farm Bill's extension through September 30, 2024, has delayed a federal resolution to the psychoactive hemp 'loophole'. States are proactively implementing stricter laws, including 'total THC' limits and bans on synthetic cannabinoids. Businesses must stay updated and adapt their operations.

specifications.federal_hemp_definition_2018_farm_bill: Cannabis sativa L. and any part of that plant, including the seeds thereof and all derivatives, extracts, cannabinoids, isomers, acids, salts, and salts of isomers, whether growing or not, with a delta-9 THC concentration of not more than 0.3 percent on a dry weight basis.
specifications.total_thc_general_concept: Calculation method used by states to include more potentially intoxicating cannabinoids (beyond just delta-9 THC) in the total THC content, often with a 0.3% limit.
specifications.total_thc_virginia_definition: Sum of all naturally occurring and synthetic tetrahydrocannabinols and tetrahydrocannabinolic acid, including their salts and isomers.

statistics.delta_8_thc_use_in_prohibition_states: More than twice as likely among past-year cannabis users compared to those in states with legal marijuana markets (study Dec 2023).
statistics.emerging_cannabinoid_use_past_year: Approximately 25% of respondents; 12% specifically delta-8 THC use.

federal.changes_2024: 2018 Farm Bill extended through Sept 30, 2024, with no new 2023 Farm Bill to address intoxicating hemp products. Next Farm Bill may address it.

california.status: Proposed (AB 2223, Feb 7, 2024) to define 'synthetically derived cannabinoid' and exclude from 'industrial hemp'. Existing (AB 45, Oct 2021) applies 'total THC' (THC + THCA) for 0.3% limit. Prohibits inhalable hemp products until regulations/tax.

nebraska.status: Proposed (LB 999, Jan 5, 2024) to clarify Hemp Farming Act didn't legalize synthetic products (esp. Delta-8 THC). Proposes shifting hemp cultivation regulation from state to USDA.

florida.status: Proposed (HB 1613, Jan 9, 2024; SB 1698, Jan 5, 2023) to limit delta-9 THC to 2mg/serving or 10mg/container. Introduces 'Total delta-9-tetrahydrocannabinol concentration' for 0.3% limit. Prohibits synthetic/naturally occurring controlled substances (delta-8, delta-10, HHC, THCA, THCP, THCV). Expands advertising/packaging restrictions.

south_dakota.status: Proposed (HB 1125, Jan 22, 2024) to ban chemically modified/converted hemp-derived products (e.g., delta-8, delta-10 THC) via 'chemically derived cannabinoid' definition.

tennessee.status: Proposed regulations (Dec 2023) to implement 'total THC' definition. Products with >~0.34% THCA likely non-compliant. Expected effective date not prior to July 1, 2024.

arkansas.status: Act 629 (2023) sought to ban various cannabinoids; enforcement blocked by federal district court (Sept 2023), currently under Eighth Circuit review.

alaska.status: New regulations effective Nov 2023. Prohibits hemp products with 'delta-9-THC or a non-naturally occurring cannabinoid' and requires 'total THC' testing. Lawsuit challenging constitutionality.

virginia.status: Amended laws effective July 2023 (passed April 2023). Limits hemp products to 0.3% 'total THC' (includes all naturally occurring and synthetic tetrahydrocannabinols/acids). Aggressive enforcement with high penalties.

compliance_requirements: Hemp businesses must stay abreast of state and federal regulatory changes in 2024.
Update relevant policies, amend operations, and implement legal strategies.
Comply with state-specific 'total THC' limits and calculation methods.
Adhere to state-specific bans or restrictions on synthetic or chemically manipulated cannabinoids (e.g., delta-8 THC, delta-10 THC, HHC, THCA, THCP, THCV in certain states).
Comply with product-specific restrictions, such as bans on inhalable hemp products in California until regulations are developed.
Observe and adhere to expanded restrictions on advertising and packaging in states like Florida.
For Nebraska, if LB 999 passes, hemp producers are advised to immediately apply for a license under the USDA production program.
e_commerce_guidance: No specific guidance for e-commerce operations was provided beyond general compliance requirements applicable to all sales channels.

references.description: Hemp Production and the 2018 Farm Bill – FDA testimony
Delta-8 THC Generated $2 Billion In Revenue In Two Years (Forbes)
Federally funded study on Δ8-THC use in states without medical or adult-use cannabis laws
South Dakota's 'chemically derived cannabinoid' definition exclusion clarification
